White Matter Development: Analysis

Benjamin Risk

5/27/2024

library(mgcv)
## Loading required package: nlme
## This is mgcv 1.8-39. For overview type 'help("mgcv-package")'.
library(itsadug)
## Loading required package: plotfunctions
## Loaded package itsadug 2.4 (see 'help("itsadug")' ).
dat_harmonized = read.csv('dat_harmonized.csv',stringsAsFactors = TRUE)

Fx – Fornix (limbic, output of hippocampus)

IFOF – inferior-fronto-occipital-fasciculus (associative, connects occipital cortex to frontal lobe)

CCb – body of corpus callosum (commissural, connects hemispheres)

PT – pyramidal tract includes pyramidal neurons connecting motor neurons to brain stem or spinal cord (corticospinal and corticobulbar tracts)

#Function for examining whether there is an interaction between GA and corrected age
### FA
maxCorrAge = function(GAlow,GAhigh=40,model,ylab,less=TRUE,print.plot=FALSE) {
  a=plot_diff(model,view='Corr_age',comp=list(GA=c(GAlow,GAhigh)),sim.ci = TRUE, hide.label=TRUE,xlab='Corrected age (days)',ylab=ylab,plot=print.plot)
  if(less) {
  max(a$Corr_age[a$est+a$sim.CI<0])
  } else {
    max(a$Corr_age[a$est-a$sim.CI>0])
  }
}
minCorrAge = function(GAlow,GAhigh=40,model,ylab,less=TRUE,print.plot=FALSE) {
  a=plot_diff(model,view='Corr_age',comp=list(GA=c(GAlow,GAhigh)),sim.ci = TRUE, hide.label=TRUE,xlab='Corrected age (days)',ylab=ylab,plot=print.plot)
  if(less) {
  min(a$Corr_age[a$est+a$sim.CI<0])
  } else {
    min(a$Corr_age[a$est-a$sim.CI>0])
  }
}

# Create plots at different gestational ages:
my.function.ga.corr <- function(dti.tract, my.ylim = c(0.1,0.3), my.ylab,  my.main="", estMaxCorrAge=TRUE,estMinCorrAge=FALSE,print.legend=FALSE,less=TRUE) {
    model0 = gam(dti.tract~ti(Corr_age,k=9)+ti(GA,k=5)+ti(Corr_age,GA,k=c(9,5))+Sex+s(Unique_ID,bs='re'),method='REML',data=dat_harmonized)
  # step into function to examine model diagnostics:
 # gam.check(model0)
  print(summary(model0))

  # vis.gam(model0,view=c('chor_age','GA'),theta=-45,ticktype='detailed',xlab='Chron Age',ylab='Gest Age',zlab='FA')
  set.seed(123)

  #tiff('../Figures/DiffCurves_FA.tiff',width=720,height=480,pointsize=20)
  if (estMaxCorrAge) {
      # par(mfrow=c(2,3))
      (ga.35=maxCorrAge(GAlow=35,GAhigh=40,model=model0,ylab='Est. difference',less=less))
      (ga.36=maxCorrAge(GAlow=36,GAhigh=40,model=model0,ylab='Est. difference',less=less))
      (ga.37=maxCorrAge(GAlow=37,GAhigh=40,model=model0,ylab='Est. difference',less=less))
      (ga.38=maxCorrAge(GAlow=38,GAhigh=40,model=model0,ylab='Est. difference',less=less))
      (ga.39=maxCorrAge(GAlow=39,GAhigh=40,model=model0,ylab='Est. difference',less=less))
      (ga.41=maxCorrAge(GAlow=40,GAhigh=41,model=model0,ylab='Est. difference',less=less))
      #dev.off()
  }
  
  if (estMinCorrAge) {
      # par(mfrow=c(2,3))
      (ga.35=minCorrAge(GAlow=35,GAhigh=40,model=model0,ylab='Est. difference',less=less))
      (ga.36=minCorrAge(GAlow=36,GAhigh=40,model=model0,ylab='Est. difference',less=less))
      (ga.37=minCorrAge(GAlow=37,GAhigh=40,model=model0,ylab='Est. difference',less=less))
      (ga.38=minCorrAge(GAlow=38,GAhigh=40,model=model0,ylab='Est. difference',less=less))
      (ga.39=minCorrAge(GAlow=39,GAhigh=40,model=model0,ylab='Est. difference',less=less))
      (ga.41=minCorrAge(GAlow=40,GAhigh=41,model=model0,ylab='Est. difference',less=less))
      #dev.off()
  }
  
  cColors = viridis::plasma(15)
  
    model0.plot = plot_smooth(model0, view="Corr_age",cond=list(GA=35),col=cColors[1],rug=FALSE,ylim=my.ylim,lwd=2,se=0,ylab=my.ylab,xlab='Corrected age (days)',hide.label=TRUE,main=my.main,xlim=c(2,201),print.summary=FALSE)
  plot_smooth(model0, view="Corr_age",cond=list(GA=36),col=cColors[3],add=TRUE,rug=FALSE,lwd=2,se=0,print.summary=FALSE)
  plot_smooth(model0, view="Corr_age",  cond=list(GA=37),col=cColors[5],add=TRUE,rug=FALSE,lwd=2,se=0,print.summary=FALSE,xlim=c(2,201))
  plot_smooth(model0, view="Corr_age", se=0, cond=list(GA=38),col=cColors[7],add=TRUE,rug=FALSE,lwd=2,print.summary=FALSE,xlim=c(2,201))
  plot_smooth(model0, view="Corr_age", se=0, cond=list(GA=39),col=cColors[9],add=TRUE,rug=FALSE,lwd=2,print.summary=FALSE,xlim=c(2,201))
  plot_smooth(model0, view="Corr_age", se=0, cond=list(GA=40),col=cColors[11],add=TRUE,rug=FALSE,lwd=2,print.summary=FALSE,xlim=c(2,201))
  plot_smooth(model0, view="Corr_age", se=0, cond=list(GA=41),col=cColors[13],add=TRUE,rug=FALSE,lwd=2,print.summary=FALSE,xlim=c(2,201))
  
  if(print.legend==TRUE) {
  legend(x='bottomright',legend=c('GA=35','GA=36','GA=37','GA=38','GA=39','GA=40','GA=41'),lty=1,lwd=2,col=cColors[seq(1,13,by=2)],bty='n',cex=1)
  }
  if (estMaxCorrAge | estMinCorrAge) {
    abline(v=ga.41,col=cColors[13],lwd=2,lty=2)
    abline(v=ga.39,col=cColors[9],lwd=2,lty=2)
    abline(v=ga.38,col=cColors[7],lwd=2,lty=2)
    abline(v=ga.37,col=cColors[5],lwd=2,lty=2)
    abline(v=ga.36,col=cColors[3],lwd=2,lty=2)
    abline(v=ga.35,col=cColors[1],lwd=2,lty=2)
  }
  my.plot = recordPlot()
  my.plot
}
tiff('../Figures/TractSpecific_GestCorrectedAgeFA.tiff',width=1080,height=1280,pointsize=18)

par(mfrow=c(4,3))

fx.fa = my.function.ga.corr(dti.tract = dat_harmonized$Fx_fa.combat, my.ylab = "FX FA")
## 
## Family: gaussian 
## Link function: identity 
## 
## Formula:
## dti.tract ~ ti(Corr_age, k = 9) + ti(GA, k = 5) + ti(Corr_age, 
##     GA, k = c(9, 5)) + Sex + s(Unique_ID, bs = "re")
## 
## Parametric coefficients:
##               Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)  0.2019474  0.0022111  91.335   <2e-16 ***
## SexM        -0.0004175  0.0028337  -0.147    0.883    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Approximate significance of smooth terms:
##                    edf Ref.df     F  p-value    
## ti(Corr_age)     2.179  2.637 9.370 8.19e-05 ***
## ti(GA)           1.237  1.312 4.044   0.0264 *  
## ti(Corr_age,GA)  1.002  1.004 0.502   0.4804    
## s(Unique_ID)    51.658 76.000 2.238 5.40e-07 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## R-sq.(adj) =  0.685   Deviance explained = 82.6%
## -REML = -368.58  Scale est. = 6.6006e-05  n = 129
## Summary:
##  * Sex : factor; set to the value(s): M. 
##  * Corr_age : numeric predictor; with 200 values ranging from 2.000000 to 201.000000. 
##  * Unique_ID : factor; set to the value(s): 00106_04. (Might be canceled as random effect, check below.) 
##  * NOTE : The following random effects columns are canceled: s(Unique_ID)
##  
##  * Simultaneous 95%-CI used : 
##      Critical value: 2.445
##      Proportion posterior simulations in pointwise CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##      Proportion posterior simulations in simultaneous CI: 1 (10000 samples)
## 
## Warning in max(a$Corr_age[a$est + a$sim.CI < 0]): no non-missing arguments to
## max; returning -Inf
## Summary:
##  * Sex : factor; set to the value(s): M. 
##  * Corr_age : numeric predictor; with 200 values ranging from 2.000000 to 201.000000. 
##  * Unique_ID : factor; set to the value(s): 00106_04. (Might be canceled as random effect, check below.) 
##  * NOTE : The following random effects columns are canceled: s(Unique_ID)
##  
##  * Simultaneous 95%-CI used : 
##      Critical value: 2.417
##      Proportion posterior simulations in pointwise CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##      Proportion posterior simulations in simultaneous CI: 1 (10000 samples)
## 
## Warning in max(a$Corr_age[a$est + a$sim.CI < 0]): no non-missing arguments to
## max; returning -Inf
## Summary:
##  * Sex : factor; set to the value(s): M. 
##  * Corr_age : numeric predictor; with 200 values ranging from 2.000000 to 201.000000. 
##  * Unique_ID : factor; set to the value(s): 00106_04. (Might be canceled as random effect, check below.) 
##  * NOTE : The following random effects columns are canceled: s(Unique_ID)
##  
##  * Simultaneous 95%-CI used : 
##      Critical value: 2.4
##      Proportion posterior simulations in pointwise CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##      Proportion posterior simulations in simultaneous CI: 1 (10000 samples)
## 
## Warning in max(a$Corr_age[a$est + a$sim.CI < 0]): no non-missing arguments to
## max; returning -Inf
## Summary:
##  * Sex : factor; set to the value(s): M. 
##  * Corr_age : numeric predictor; with 200 values ranging from 2.000000 to 201.000000. 
##  * Unique_ID : factor; set to the value(s): 00106_04. (Might be canceled as random effect, check below.) 
##  * NOTE : The following random effects columns are canceled: s(Unique_ID)
##  
##  * Simultaneous 95%-CI used : 
##      Critical value: 2.351
##      Proportion posterior simulations in pointwise CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##      Proportion posterior simulations in simultaneous CI: 1 (10000 samples)
## 
## Warning in max(a$Corr_age[a$est + a$sim.CI < 0]): no non-missing arguments to
## max; returning -Inf
## Summary:
##  * Sex : factor; set to the value(s): M. 
##  * Corr_age : numeric predictor; with 200 values ranging from 2.000000 to 201.000000. 
##  * Unique_ID : factor; set to the value(s): 00106_04. (Might be canceled as random effect, check below.) 
##  * NOTE : The following random effects columns are canceled: s(Unique_ID)
##  
##  * Simultaneous 95%-CI used : 
##      Critical value: 2.361
##      Proportion posterior simulations in pointwise CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##      Proportion posterior simulations in simultaneous CI: 1 (10000 samples)
## 
## Warning in max(a$Corr_age[a$est + a$sim.CI < 0]): no non-missing arguments to

## Summary:
##  * Sex : factor; set to the value(s): M. 
##  * Corr_age : numeric predictor; with 200 values ranging from 2.000000 to 201.000000. 
##  * Unique_ID : factor; set to the value(s): 00106_04. (Might be canceled as random effect, check below.)

ci.fa = my.function.ga.corr(dti.tract = dat_harmonized$Ci_fa.combat, my.ylab = "Ci FA")
## 
## Family: gaussian 
## Link function: identity 
## 
## Formula:
## dti.tract ~ ti(Corr_age, k = 9) + ti(GA, k = 5) + ti(Corr_age, 
##     GA, k = c(9, 5)) + Sex + s(Unique_ID, bs = "re")
## 
## Parametric coefficients:
##             Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept) 0.162877   0.001255 129.771   <2e-16 ***
## SexM        0.001885   0.001608   1.172    0.245    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

ccg.fa = my.function.ga.corr(dti.tract = dat_harmonized$CCg_fa.combat, my.ylab = "CCg FA")
## 
## Family: gaussian 
## Link function: identity 
## 
## Formula:
## dti.tract ~ ti(Corr_age, k = 9) + ti(GA, k = 5) + ti(Corr_age, 
##     GA, k = c(9, 5)) + Sex + s(Unique_ID, bs = "re")
## 
## Parametric coefficients:
##               Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)  0.1807556  0.0015645 115.536   <2e-16 ***
## SexM        -0.0009693  0.0019988  -0.485    0.629    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Approximate significance of smooth terms:
##                    edf Ref.df       F  p-value    
## ti(Corr_age)     3.162  3.784 106.862  < 2e-16 ***
## ti(GA)           1.000  1.000   0.162 0.688601    
## ti(Corr_age,GA)  1.039  1.067   1.875 0.181480    
## s(Unique_ID)    38.108 76.000   1.092 0.000295 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## R-sq.(adj) =  0.843   Deviance explained = 89.7%
## -REML = -398.62  Scale est. = 5.3221e-05  n = 129
## Summary:
##  * Sex : factor; set to the value(s): M. 
##  * Corr_age : numeric predictor; with 200 values ranging from 2.000000 to 201.000000. 
##  * Unique_ID : factor; set to the value(s): 00106_04. (Might be canceled as random effect, check below.) 
##  * NOTE : The following random effects columns are canceled: s(Unique_ID)
##  
##  * Simultaneous 95%-CI used : 
##      Critical value: 2.601
##      Proportion posterior simulations in pointwise CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##      Proportion posterior simulations in simultaneous CI: 1 (10000 samples)
## 
## Warning in max(a$Corr_age[a$est + a$sim.CI < 0]): no non-missing arguments to
## max; returning -Inf
## Summary:
##  * Sex : factor; set to the value(s): M. 
##  * Corr_age : numeric predictor; with 200 values ranging from 2.000000 to 201.000000. 
##  * Unique_ID : factor; set to the value(s): 00106_04. (Might be canceled as random effect, check below.) 
##  * NOTE : The following random effects columns are canceled: s(Unique_ID)
##  
##  * Simultaneous 95%-CI used : 
##      Critical value: 2.59
##      Proportion posterior simulations in pointwise CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##      Proportion posterior simulations in simultaneous CI: 1 (10000 samples)
## 
## Warning in max(a$Corr_age[a$est + a$sim.CI < 0]): no non-missing arguments to
## max; returning -Inf
## Summary:

##  * Corr_age : numeric predictor; with 200 values ranging from 2.000000 to 201.000000. 
##  * Unique_ID : factor; set to the value(s): 00106_04. (Might be canceled as random effect, check below.) 
##  * NOTE : The following random effects columns are canceled: s(Unique_ID)
##  
##  * Simultaneous 95%-CI used : 
##      Critical value: 2.621
##      Proportion posterior simulations in pointwise CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##      Proportion posterior simulations in simultaneous CI: 1 (10000 samples)
## 
## Warning in max(a$Corr_age[a$est + a$sim.CI < 0]): no non-missing arguments to
## max; returning -Inf
## Summary:
##  * Sex : factor; set to the value(s): M. 
##  * Corr_age : numeric predictor; with 200 values ranging from 2.000000 to 201.000000. 
##  * Unique_ID : factor; set to the value(s): 00106_04. (Might be canceled as random effect, check below.) 
##  * NOTE : The following random effects columns are canceled: s(Unique_ID)
##  
##  * Simultaneous 95%-CI used : 
##      Critical value: 2.582
##      Proportion posterior simulations in pointwise CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##      Proportion posterior simulations in simultaneous CI: 1 (10000 samples)

## Warning in max(a$Corr_age[a$est + a$sim.CI < 0]): no non-missing arguments to

##      Proportion posterior simulations in simultaneous CI: 1 (10000 samples)

## max; returning -Inf
## Summary:
##  * Sex : factor; set to the value(s): M. 
##  * Corr_age : numeric predictor; with 200 values ranging from 2.000000 to 201.000000. 
##  * Unique_ID : factor; set to the value(s): 00106_04. (Might be canceled as random effect, check below.)

##      Critical value: 2.613
##      Proportion posterior simulations in pointwise CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##      Proportion posterior simulations in simultaneous CI: 1 (10000 samples)

## max; returning -Inf
ccb.fa = my.function.ga.corr(dti.tract = dat_harmonized$CCb_fa.combat, my.ylab="CCb FA")
## 
## Family: gaussian 
## Link function: identity 
## 
## Formula:
## dti.tract ~ ti(Corr_age, k = 9) + ti(GA, k = 5) + ti(Corr_age, 
##     GA, k = c(9, 5)) + Sex + s(Unique_ID, bs = "re")
## 
## Parametric coefficients:
##              Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)  0.215074   0.001863 115.443   <2e-16 ***
## SexM        -0.001608   0.002390  -0.673    0.503    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Approximate significance of smooth terms:
##                    edf Ref.df       F p-value    
## ti(Corr_age)     3.960  4.633 145.730  <2e-16 ***
## ti(GA)           1.000  1.000   0.418   0.520    
## ti(Corr_age,GA)  1.001  1.001   0.031   0.861    
## s(Unique_ID)    54.541 76.000   2.846  <2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## R-sq.(adj) =  0.916   Deviance explained = 95.6%
## -REML = -390.96  Scale est. = 4.0576e-05  n = 129
## Summary:
##  * Sex : factor; set to the value(s): M. 
##  * Corr_age : numeric predictor; with 200 values ranging from 2.000000 to 201.000000.

ccs.fa = my.function.ga.corr(dti.tract = dat_harmonized$CCs_fa.combat, my.ylab="CCs FA")
## 
## Family: gaussian 
## Link function: identity 
## 
## Formula:
## dti.tract ~ ti(Corr_age, k = 9) + ti(GA, k = 5) + ti(Corr_age, 
##     GA, k = c(9, 5)) + Sex + s(Unique_ID, bs = "re")
## 
## Parametric coefficients:
##              Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)  0.169067   0.001922  87.969   <2e-16 ***
## SexM        -0.001511   0.002461  -0.614    0.541    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Approximate significance of smooth terms:
##                    edf Ref.df      F p-value    
## ti(Corr_age)     2.989  3.574 70.870 < 2e-16 ***
## ti(GA)           1.000  1.000  0.676   0.414    
## ti(Corr_age,GA)  1.000  1.001  0.135   0.715    
## s(Unique_ID)    47.284 76.000  1.836 2.6e-06 ***

##  * Corr_age : numeric predictor; with 200 values ranging from 2.000000 to 201.000000. 
##  * Unique_ID : factor; set to the value(s): 00106_04. (Might be canceled as random effect, check below.) 
##  * NOTE : The following random effects columns are canceled: s(Unique_ID)
##  
##  * Simultaneous 95%-CI used : 
##      Critical value: 2.378
##      Proportion posterior simulations in pointwise CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##      Proportion posterior simulations in simultaneous CI: 1 (10000 samples)
## 
## Warning in max(a$Corr_age[a$est + a$sim.CI < 0]): no non-missing arguments to
## max; returning -Inf
uf.fa = my.function.ga.corr(dti.tract = dat_harmonized$UF_fa.combat, my.ylab="UF FA")
## 
## Family: gaussian 
## Link function: identity 
## 
## Formula:
## dti.tract ~ ti(Corr_age, k = 9) + ti(GA, k = 5) + ti(Corr_age, 
##     GA, k = c(9, 5)) + Sex + s(Unique_ID, bs = "re")

## Parametric coefficients:
##               Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)  1.727e-01  1.536e-03 112.474   <2e-16 ***
## SexM        -3.257e-05  1.966e-03  -0.017    0.987    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

## ti(Corr_age,GA)  1.001  1.002  1.814    0.182

plot.new()
  cColors = viridis::plasma(15)
  
legend(x='topleft',legend=c('GA=35','GA=36','GA=37','GA=38','GA=39','GA=40','GA=41'),lty=1,lwd=2,col=cColors[seq(1,13,by=2)],bty='n',cex=1.2)

dev.off()
## png 
##   2

MD

tiff('../Figures/TractSpecific_GestCorrectedAgeMD.tiff',width=1080,height=1280,pointsize=18)

par(mfrow=c(4,3))

fx = my.function.ga.corr(dti.tract = dat_harmonized$Fx_md.combat, my.ylab = "FX MD",less=FALSE,my.ylim = c(0.9,1.5),estMinCorrAge = FALSE,estMaxCorrAge=FALSE)

## Link function: identity

ccg = my.function.ga.corr(dti.tract = dat_harmonized$CCg_md.combat, my.ylab = "CCg MD",my.ylim = c(0.9,1.5),less=FALSE)
## 
## Family: gaussian 
## Link function: identity 
## 
## Formula:
## dti.tract ~ ti(Corr_age, k = 9) + ti(GA, k = 5) + ti(Corr_age, 
##     GA, k = c(9, 5)) + Sex + s(Unique_ID, bs = "re")
## 
## Parametric coefficients:
##             Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)  1.18197    0.01371  86.206   <2e-16 ***
## SexM         0.01004    0.01755   0.572    0.569    
## ---

##  * Unique_ID : factor; set to the value(s): 00106_04. (Might be canceled as random effect, check below.)

##      Critical value: 2.909
##      Proportion posterior simulations in pointwise CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##      Proportion posterior simulations in simultaneous CI: 1 (10000 samples)

##  * Sex : factor; set to the value(s): M.

af = my.function.ga.corr(dti.tract = dat_harmonized$AF_md.combat, my.ylab = "AF MD",my.ylim=c(0.9,1.5),less=FALSE,estMaxCorrAge=FALSE)
## 
## Family: gaussian 
## Link function: identity 
## 
## Formula:
## dti.tract ~ ti(Corr_age, k = 9) + ti(GA, k = 5) + ti(Corr_age, 
##     GA, k = c(9, 5)) + Sex + s(Unique_ID, bs = "re")
## 
## Parametric coefficients:
##             Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept) 1.041801   0.007817 133.282   <2e-16 ***
## SexM        0.007655   0.010000   0.766    0.446    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Approximate significance of smooth terms:
##                    edf Ref.df      F p-value    
## ti(Corr_age)     3.749  4.435 62.799 < 2e-16 ***
## ti(GA)           1.000  1.000  0.051 0.82185    
## ti(Corr_age,GA)  2.531  2.940  3.515 0.03102 *  
## s(Unique_ID)    35.765 76.000  0.934 0.00116 ** 
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## R-sq.(adj) =  0.782   Deviance explained = 85.7%
## -REML = -195.73  Scale est. = 0.0013957  n = 129
uf = my.function.ga.corr(dti.tract = dat_harmonized$UF_md.combat, my.ylab="UF MD",less=FALSE,my.ylim=c(0.9,1.5))

pt = my.function.ga.corr(dti.tract = dat_harmonized$PT_md.combat, my.ylab="PT MD",print.legend = FALSE,less=FALSE,estMinCorrAge=FALSE,estMaxCorrAge=FALSE,my.ylim=c(0.9,1.5))
## 
## Family: gaussian 
## Link function: identity 
## 
## Formula:
## dti.tract ~ ti(Corr_age, k = 9) + ti(GA, k = 5) + ti(Corr_age, 
##     GA, k = c(9, 5)) + Sex + s(Unique_ID, bs = "re")
## 
## Parametric coefficients:
##             Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)  1.04393    0.01150  90.813   <2e-16 ***
## SexM         0.01740    0.01478   1.177    0.243    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Approximate significance of smooth terms:
##                    edf Ref.df      F  p-value    
## ti(Corr_age)     1.794  2.167 29.971  < 2e-16 ***
## ti(GA)           2.602  2.811  2.732   0.0537 .  
## ti(Corr_age,GA)  2.335  2.913  0.677   0.5228    
## s(Unique_ID)    43.721 76.000  1.463 2.69e-05 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## R-sq.(adj) =  0.656   Deviance explained = 79.4%
## -REML = -156.57  Scale est. = 0.0022956  n = 129
plot.new()
  cColors = viridis::plasma(15)
  
legend(x='topleft',legend=c('GA=35','GA=36','GA=37','GA=38','GA=39','GA=40','GA=41'),lty=1,lwd=2,col=cColors[seq(1,13,by=2)],bty='n',cex=1.2)

dev.off()
## png 
##   2

AD

tiff('../Figures/TractSpecific_GestCorrectedAgeAD.tiff',width=1080,height=1280,pointsize=18)

par(mfrow=c(4,3))

## 
## Family: gaussian 
## Link function: identity

##      Proportion posterior simulations in simultaneous CI: 1 (10000 samples)
## 
## Warning in max(a$Corr_age[a$est - a$sim.CI > 0]): no non-missing arguments to

## Summary:

## 
## Family: gaussian 
## Link function: identity 
## 
## Formula:
## dti.tract ~ ti(Corr_age, k = 9) + ti(GA, k = 5) + ti(Corr_age, 
##     GA, k = c(9, 5)) + Sex + s(Unique_ID, bs = "re")
## 
## Parametric coefficients:
##             Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept) 1.238982   0.008209 150.929   <2e-16 ***
## SexM        0.007895   0.010510   0.751    0.455    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Approximate significance of smooth terms:
##                    edf Ref.df      F  p-value    
## ti(Corr_age)     3.473  4.122 47.045  < 2e-16 ***
## ti(GA)           1.000  1.000  0.004 0.946808    
## ti(Corr_age,GA)  2.536  2.942  3.458 0.033029 *  
## s(Unique_ID)    38.579 76.000  1.092 0.000396 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## R-sq.(adj) =  0.738   Deviance explained = 83.3%
## -REML = -192.04  Scale est. = 0.0014226  n = 129
uf = my.function.ga.corr(dti.tract = dat_harmonized$UF_ad.combat, my.ylab="UF AD",less=FALSE,my.ylim = c(1.1,1.7))
## 
## Family: gaussian 
## Link function: identity 
## 
## Formula:

pt = my.function.ga.corr(dti.tract = dat_harmonized$PT_ad.combat, my.ylab="PT AD",print.legend = FALSE,less=FALSE,estMinCorrAge=FALSE,estMaxCorrAge=FALSE,my.ylim = c(1.1,1.7))
## 
## Family: gaussian 
## Link function: identity 
## 
## Formula:
## dti.tract ~ ti(Corr_age, k = 9) + ti(GA, k = 5) + ti(Corr_age, 
##     GA, k = c(9, 5)) + Sex + s(Unique_ID, bs = "re")
## 
## Parametric coefficients:
##             Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)  1.34285    0.01184 113.406   <2e-16 ***
## SexM         0.01890    0.01521   1.242    0.218    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Approximate significance of smooth terms:
##                    edf Ref.df      F  p-value    
## ti(Corr_age)     1.001  1.001 39.807  < 2e-16 ***
## ti(GA)           2.667  2.885  2.843   0.0463 *  
## ti(Corr_age,GA)  2.037  2.550  0.356   0.6977    
## s(Unique_ID)    42.064 76.000  1.310 8.68e-05 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## R-sq.(adj) =  0.595   Deviance explained = 74.9%
## -REML = -151.98  Scale est. = 0.0025982  n = 129
plot.new()
  cColors = viridis::plasma(15)
  
legend(x='topleft',legend=c('GA=35','GA=36','GA=37','GA=38','GA=39','GA=40','GA=41'),lty=1,lwd=2,col=cColors[seq(1,13,by=2)],bty='n',cex=1.2)

dev.off()
## png 
##   2

AF is strange: small region over which there are significant differences, approximately 50-70 days. For clarify, don’t plus the significance lines.

RD

tiff('../Figures/TractSpecific_GestCorrectedAgeRD.tiff',width=1080,height=1280,pointsize=18)
par(mfrow=c(4,3))

fx = my.function.ga.corr(dti.tract = dat_harmonized$Fx_rd.combat, my.ylab = "FX RD",less=FALSE,my.ylim = c(0.8,1.4),estMinCorrAge = FALSE,estMaxCorrAge=FALSE)

## -REML = -195.06  Scale est. = 0.0014346  n = 129
## Summary:
##  * Sex : factor; set to the value(s): M. 
##  * Corr_age : numeric predictor; with 200 values ranging from 2.000000 to 201.000000. 
##  * Unique_ID : factor; set to the value(s): 00106_04. (Might be canceled as random effect, check below.)

## 
## Family: gaussian

pt = my.function.ga.corr(dti.tract = dat_harmonized$PT_rd.combat, my.ylab="PT RD",print.legend = FALSE,less=FALSE,estMinCorrAge=FALSE,estMaxCorrAge=FALSE,my.ylim = c(0.8,1.4))
## 
## Family: gaussian 
## Link function: identity 
## 
## Formula:
## dti.tract ~ ti(Corr_age, k = 9) + ti(GA, k = 5) + ti(Corr_age, 
##     GA, k = c(9, 5)) + Sex + s(Unique_ID, bs = "re")
## 
## Parametric coefficients:
##             Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)  0.89447    0.01146  78.040   <2e-16 ***
## SexM         0.01660    0.01473   1.127    0.263    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Approximate significance of smooth terms:
##                    edf Ref.df      F p-value    
## ti(Corr_age)     2.058  2.485 31.868 < 2e-16 ***
## ti(GA)           2.548  2.752  2.623  0.0622 .  
## ti(Corr_age,GA)  2.527  3.170  0.835  0.4717    
## s(Unique_ID)    44.614 76.000  1.555 1.5e-05 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## R-sq.(adj) =  0.684   Deviance explained = 81.4%
## -REML = -157.48  Scale est. = 0.0022039  n = 129
plot.new()
  cColors = viridis::plasma(15)
  
legend(x='topleft',legend=c('GA=35','GA=36','GA=37','GA=38','GA=39','GA=40','GA=41'),lty=1,lwd=2,col=cColors[seq(1,13,by=2)],bty='n',cex=1.2)

dev.off()
## png 
##   2
tiff('../Figures/TractSpecific_GestCorrectedAgeRD.tiff',width=1080,height=1280,pointsize=18)
par(mfrow=c(4,3))

#pdf(file='../Figures/TractSpecific_GestCorrectedAgeRD.pdf')

## 
## Family: gaussian 
## Link function: identity 
## 
## Formula:
## dti.tract ~ ti(Corr_age, k = 9) + ti(GA, k = 5) + ti(Corr_age, 
##     GA, k = c(9, 5)) + Sex + s(Unique_ID, bs = "re")
## 
## Parametric coefficients:
##             Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept) 1.068501   0.013691  78.043   <2e-16 ***
## SexM        0.009499   0.017497   0.543    0.589    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
##

##                    edf Ref.df      F  p-value    
## ti(Corr_age)     1.303  1.504 67.077  < 2e-16 ***
## ti(GA)           1.870  2.112  1.838 0.183515    
## ti(Corr_age,GA)  1.391  1.634  1.054 0.420397    
## s(Unique_ID)    38.162 76.000  1.051 0.000493 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## R-sq.(adj) =  0.666   Deviance explained =   78%
## -REML = -131.44  Scale est. = 0.0040189  n = 129
## Summary:

## Family: gaussian 
## Link function: identity

## Formula:

##  
##  * Simultaneous 95%-CI used : 
##      Critical value: 2.799
##      Proportion posterior simulations in pointwise CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##      Proportion posterior simulations in simultaneous CI: 1 (10000 samples)
## 
## Warning in max(a$Corr_age[a$est - a$sim.CI > 0]): no non-missing arguments to
## max; returning -Inf
af = my.function.ga.corr(dti.tract = dat_harmonized$AF_rd.combat, my.ylab = "AF RD",my.ylim = c(0.8,1.4),less=FALSE)
## 
## Family: gaussian 
## Link function: identity 
## 
## Formula:
## dti.tract ~ ti(Corr_age, k = 9) + ti(GA, k = 5) + ti(Corr_age, 
##     GA, k = c(9, 5)) + Sex + s(Unique_ID, bs = "re")
## 
## Parametric coefficients:
##             Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept) 0.943169   0.007805  120.84   <2e-16 ***
## SexM        0.007588   0.009982    0.76    0.449    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Approximate significance of smooth terms:
##                    edf Ref.df      F p-value    
## ti(Corr_age)     3.844  4.543 69.510 < 2e-16 ***
## ti(GA)           1.000  1.000  0.082 0.77494    
## ti(Corr_age,GA)  2.493  2.903  3.453 0.03744 *  
## s(Unique_ID)    34.640 76.000  0.878 0.00171 ** 
## ---

## 
## Family: gaussian 
## Link function: identity 
## 
## Formula:
## dti.tract ~ ti(Corr_age, k = 9) + ti(GA, k = 5) + ti(Corr_age, 
##     GA, k = c(9, 5)) + Sex + s(Unique_ID, bs = "re")

atr = my.function.ga.corr(dti.tract = dat_harmonized$ATR_rd.combat, my.ylab="ATR RD",less=FALSE,my.ylim = c(0.8,1.4))
## 
## Family: gaussian 
## Link function: identity 
## 
## Formula:
## dti.tract ~ ti(Corr_age, k = 9) + ti(GA, k = 5) + ti(Corr_age, 
##     GA, k = c(9, 5)) + Sex + s(Unique_ID, bs = "re")
## 
## Parametric coefficients:
##             Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept) 0.917249   0.008631 106.272   <2e-16 ***
## SexM        0.011819   0.011079   1.067     0.29    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Approximate significance of smooth terms:
##                    edf Ref.df      F  p-value    
## ti(Corr_age)     4.237  4.923 38.385  < 2e-16 ***
## ti(GA)           1.000  1.000  0.017  0.89669    
## ti(Corr_age,GA)  3.309  3.649  4.995  0.00281 ** 
## s(Unique_ID)    48.402 76.000  1.921 2.68e-06 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## R-sq.(adj) =  0.809   Deviance explained = 89.5%
## -REML = -191.74  Scale est. = 0.0011053  n = 129
## Summary:
##  * Sex : factor; set to the value(s): M. 
##  * Corr_age : numeric predictor; with 200 values ranging from 2.000000 to 201.000000. 
##  * Unique_ID : factor; set to the value(s): 00106_04. (Might be canceled as random effect, check below.) 
##  * NOTE : The following random effects columns are canceled: s(Unique_ID)
##  
##  * Simultaneous 95%-CI used : 
##      Critical value: 2.377
##      Proportion posterior simulations in pointwise CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##      Proportion posterior simulations in simultaneous CI: 1 (10000 samples)
## 
## Warning in max(a$Corr_age[a$est - a$sim.CI > 0]): no non-missing arguments to
## max; returning -Inf
## Summary:
##  * Sex : factor; set to the value(s): M. 
##  * Corr_age : numeric predictor; with 200 values ranging from 2.000000 to 201.000000. 
##  * Unique_ID : factor; set to the value(s): 00106_04. (Might be canceled as random effect, check below.) 
##  * NOTE : The following random effects columns are canceled: s(Unique_ID)
##  
##  * Simultaneous 95%-CI used : 
##      Critical value: 2.391
##      Proportion posterior simulations in pointwise CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##      Proportion posterior simulations in simultaneous CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##  
## Summary:
##  * Sex : factor; set to the value(s): M. 
##  * Corr_age : numeric predictor; with 200 values ranging from 2.000000 to 201.000000. 
##  * Unique_ID : factor; set to the value(s): 00106_04. (Might be canceled as random effect, check below.) 
##  * NOTE : The following random effects columns are canceled: s(Unique_ID)
##  
##  * Simultaneous 95%-CI used : 
##      Critical value: 2.417
##      Proportion posterior simulations in pointwise CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##      Proportion posterior simulations in simultaneous CI: 1 (10000 samples)
## 
## Warning in max(a$Corr_age[a$est - a$sim.CI > 0]): no non-missing arguments to
## max; returning -Inf
## Summary:
##  * Sex : factor; set to the value(s): M. 
##  * Corr_age : numeric predictor; with 200 values ranging from 2.000000 to 201.000000. 
##  * Unique_ID : factor; set to the value(s): 00106_04. (Might be canceled as random effect, check below.) 
##  * NOTE : The following random effects columns are canceled: s(Unique_ID)
##  
##  * Simultaneous 95%-CI used : 
##      Critical value: 2.43
##      Proportion posterior simulations in pointwise CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##      Proportion posterior simulations in simultaneous CI: 1 (10000 samples)
## 
## Warning in max(a$Corr_age[a$est - a$sim.CI > 0]): no non-missing arguments to
## max; returning -Inf
## Summary:
##  * Sex : factor; set to the value(s): M. 
##  * Corr_age : numeric predictor; with 200 values ranging from 2.000000 to 201.000000. 
##  * Unique_ID : factor; set to the value(s): 00106_04. (Might be canceled as random effect, check below.) 
##  * NOTE : The following random effects columns are canceled: s(Unique_ID)
##  
##  * Simultaneous 95%-CI used : 
##      Critical value: 2.449
##      Proportion posterior simulations in pointwise CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##      Proportion posterior simulations in simultaneous CI: 1 (10000 samples)
## 
## Warning in max(a$Corr_age[a$est - a$sim.CI > 0]): no non-missing arguments to
## max; returning -Inf
## Summary:
##  * Sex : factor; set to the value(s): M. 
##  * Corr_age : numeric predictor; with 200 values ranging from 2.000000 to 201.000000. 
##  * Unique_ID : factor; set to the value(s): 00106_04. (Might be canceled as random effect, check below.) 
##  * NOTE : The following random effects columns are canceled: s(Unique_ID)
##  
##  * Simultaneous 95%-CI used : 
##      Critical value: 2.444
##      Proportion posterior simulations in pointwise CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##      Proportion posterior simulations in simultaneous CI: 1 (10000 samples)
## 
## Warning in max(a$Corr_age[a$est - a$sim.CI > 0]): no non-missing arguments to
## max; returning -Inf
pt = my.function.ga.corr(dti.tract = dat_harmonized$PT_rd.combat, my.ylab="PT RD",print.legend = FALSE,less=FALSE,estMinCorrAge=FALSE,estMaxCorrAge=FALSE,my.ylim = c(0.8,1.4))
## 
## Family: gaussian 
## Link function: identity 
## 
## Formula:
## dti.tract ~ ti(Corr_age, k = 9) + ti(GA, k = 5) + ti(Corr_age, 
##     GA, k = c(9, 5)) + Sex + s(Unique_ID, bs = "re")
## 
## Parametric coefficients:
##             Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)  0.89447    0.01146  78.040   <2e-16 ***
## SexM         0.01660    0.01473   1.127    0.263    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Approximate significance of smooth terms:
##                    edf Ref.df      F p-value    
## ti(Corr_age)     2.058  2.485 31.868 < 2e-16 ***
## ti(GA)           2.548  2.752  2.623  0.0622 .  
## ti(Corr_age,GA)  2.527  3.170  0.835  0.4717    
## s(Unique_ID)    44.614 76.000  1.555 1.5e-05 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## R-sq.(adj) =  0.684   Deviance explained = 81.4%
## -REML = -157.48  Scale est. = 0.0022039  n = 129
plot.new()
  cColors = viridis::plasma(15)
  
legend(x='topleft',legend=c('GA=35','GA=36','GA=37','GA=38','GA=39','GA=40','GA=41'),lty=1,lwd=2,col=cColors[seq(1,13,by=2)],bty='n',cex=1.2)

dev.off()
## png 
##   2

Additional inspection of models

Check the simultaneous confidenc bands for MD ATR, where the interaction GA*Corrected age was p=0.003:

model.atr.md = gam(ATR_md.combat~ti(Corr_age,k=9)+ti(GA,k=5)+ti(Corr_age,GA,k=c(9,5))+Sex+s(Unique_ID,bs='re'),method='REML',data=dat_harmonized)
plot_diff(model.atr.md,view='Corr_age',comp=list(GA=c(35,40)),sim.ci = TRUE,xlab='Corrected age (days)')
## Summary:
##  * Sex : factor; set to the value(s): M. 
##  * Corr_age : numeric predictor; with 200 values ranging from 2.000000 to 201.000000. 
##  * Unique_ID : factor; set to the value(s): 00106_04. (Might be canceled as random effect, check below.) 
##  * NOTE : The following random effects columns are canceled: s(Unique_ID)
##  
##  * Simultaneous 95%-CI used : 
##      Critical value: 2.412
##      Proportion posterior simulations in pointwise CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##      Proportion posterior simulations in simultaneous CI: 1 (10000 samples)
## 

## 
## Difference is not significant.

Conclusion: Gestational age has little effect. In ATR, the approximate pvalue is significant, but the difference plot shows little effect. The estimated effect is a bit hard to interpret: at age corrected 0 days, GA=35 are more developed (lower MD), but by 200 days, they are less developed. Note: also checked without sex, and result did not change.

In MD PT, main effect of GA has p=0.05 in tensor spline model.

summary(gam(PT_md.combat~s(Corr_age,k=9)+s(GA,k=5)+ti(Corr_age,GA,k=c(9,5))+s(Unique_ID,bs='re'),method='REML',data=dat_harmonized))
## 
## Family: gaussian 
## Link function: identity 
## 
## Formula:
## PT_md.combat ~ s(Corr_age, k = 9) + s(GA, k = 5) + ti(Corr_age, 
##     GA, k = c(9, 5)) + s(Unique_ID, bs = "re")
## 
## Parametric coefficients:
##             Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept) 1.054417   0.007207   146.3   <2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Approximate significance of smooth terms:
##                    edf Ref.df      F  p-value    
## s(Corr_age)      1.788  2.162 29.793  < 2e-16 ***
## s(GA)            2.555  2.775  2.731   0.0539 .  
## ti(Corr_age,GA)  2.201  2.739  0.437   0.6441    
## s(Unique_ID)    44.305 77.000  1.457 2.84e-05 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## R-sq.(adj) =  0.653   Deviance explained = 79.1%
## -REML = -156.3  Scale est. = 0.0023158  n = 129

Check if GA is significant when there is not bivariate spline:

model.pt.md = gam(PT_md.combat~s(Corr_age,k=9)+s(GA,k=5)+s(Unique_ID,bs='re'),method='REML',data=dat_harmonized)
plot_diff(model.pt.md,view='Corr_age',comp=list(GA=c(35,40)),sim.ci = TRUE,xlab='Corrected age (days)')
## Summary:
##  * Corr_age : numeric predictor; with 200 values ranging from 2.000000 to 201.000000. 
##  * Unique_ID : factor; set to the value(s): 00106_04. (Might be canceled as random effect, check below.) 
##  * NOTE : The following random effects columns are canceled: s(Unique_ID)
##  
##  * Simultaneous 95%-CI used : 
##      Critical value: 1.927
##      Proportion posterior simulations in pointwise CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##      Proportion posterior simulations in simultaneous CI: 1 (10000 samples)
## 

## 
## Difference is not significant.
summary(model.pt.md)
## 
## Family: gaussian 
## Link function: identity 
## 
## Formula:
## PT_md.combat ~ s(Corr_age, k = 9) + s(GA, k = 5) + s(Unique_ID, 
##     bs = "re")
## 
## Parametric coefficients:
##             Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept) 1.054156   0.007125   147.9   <2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Approximate significance of smooth terms:
##                 edf Ref.df      F  p-value    
## s(Corr_age)   1.717  2.064 32.481  < 2e-16 ***
## s(GA)         2.436  2.654  3.197   0.0193 *  
## s(Unique_ID) 44.550 77.000  1.449 3.18e-05 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## R-sq.(adj) =  0.645   Deviance explained =   78%
## -REML = -157.38  Scale est. = 0.0023679  n = 129
# step into function to examine model diagnostics:
gam.check(model.pt.md)

## 
## Method: REML   Optimizer: outer newton
## full convergence after 6 iterations.
## Gradient range [-1.976485e-07,1.274147e-07]
## (score -157.3772 & scale 0.002367946).
## Hessian positive definite, eigenvalue range [0.1971956,71.39753].
## Model rank =  92 / 92 
## 
## Basis dimension (k) checking results. Low p-value (k-index<1) may
## indicate that k is too low, especially if edf is close to k'.
## 
##                 k'   edf k-index p-value
## s(Corr_age)   8.00  1.72    1.08    0.80
## s(GA)         4.00  2.44    1.11    0.91
## s(Unique_ID) 79.00 44.55      NA      NA

Simultaneous confidence intervals broadly overlapping. The approximate p value of GA is smaller (p=0.02), but I defer to the simultaneous confidence bands.
